Title: The Rice Abscisic Acid-Responsive RING Finger E3 Ligase OsRF1 Targets OsPP2C09 for Degradation and Confers Drought and Salinity Tolerance in Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsRF1 corresponds to LOC_Os09g30160; a codon-optimized synthetic ORF (75.5% nucleotide identity but identical amino-acid sequence to native OsRF1) was used to generate overexpression lines. OsRF1 contains a predicted N-terminal transmembrane domain enriched in protease cleavage sites, and GFP fusions localize mainly to ER membranes and perinuclear regions. Overexpression causes semi-dwarfism and enhanced seed dormancy in the field.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsRF1 encodes a small ER-anchored RING-H2 E3 ubiquitin ligase whose transcript is strongly induced by ABA, drought and salt stress.
2. Overexpression of OsRF1 in rice elevates expression of ABA biosynthetic genes (ZEP, ABA4, NCED3/ABA3), increases endogenous ABA levels, and causes ABA-hypersensitive inhibition of shoot growth, reduced GA sensitivity, semi-dwarf growth and enhanced seed dormancy.
3. OsRF1-OE plants show improved drought and salinity tolerance and reduced water loss at leaf and whole-plant levels, correlating with higher ABA accumulation.
4. OsRF1 directly interacts with clade A PP2Cs (especially OsPP2C09) and mediates their polyubiquitination and 26S proteasome-dependent degradation, thereby relieving PP2C inhibition of ABA signaling and indirectly stabilizing the DELLA protein SLR1 to dampen GA-induced growth under stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsRF1 overexpression enhances drought and salt tolerance and reduces leaf and whole-plant water loss by elevating ABA biosynthesis/signaling, while simultaneously restraining GA-dependent shoot elongation through stabilization of the DELLA protein SLR1, resulting in ABA-hypersensitive, semi-dwarf plants with improved stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a RING-H2 E3 ubiquitin ligase that self-ubiquitinates and polyubiquitinates clade A PP2Cs (especially OsPP2C09), targeting them for 26S proteasome-mediated degradation in an ABA- and stress-dependent manner, thereby relieving PP2C inhibition of ABA signaling and indirectly stabilizing SLR1 to attenuate GA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling pathway and ABA–GA signaling crosstalk in the regulation of drought and salinity responses and growth restraint via SLR1.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsRF1 --(activation of transcription under stress)--> ABA biosynthetic genes ZEP, ABA4 (NSY), NCED3, ABA3 (faster and stronger induction under drought/salt in OsRF1-OE than WT); OsRF1 --(E3 ligase-mediated polyubiquitination and 26S proteasome-dependent degradation)--> OsPP2C09 (and OsPP2C50); degradation of OsPP2C09 -->(activation)--> ABA signaling; enhanced ABA signaling -->(stabilization)--> SLR1 (DELLA) -->(repression)--> GA-dependent shoot elongation and promotion of seed dormancy.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small membrane-anchored RING-H2 type RING finger protein containing a canonical RING-H2 domain and an N-terminal transmembrane segment.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predominantly localized to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) membrane, cytoplasm and peripheral region of the nucleus in rice protoplasts; GFP-OsRF1 co-localizes with the ER mCherry marker CD3-960.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Clade A PP2Cs: strong direct interaction with OsPP2C09 (Y2H, BiFC in nucleus, co-IP in rice protoplasts); interaction with OsPP2C50 (Y2H, BiFC in cytoplasm, substrate in in vitro ubiquitination). Weaker Y2H interaction signals were also observed with OsPP2C08, OsPP2C30 and OsPP2C53.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsRF1 expression in 10-day-old shoots is induced ~3.25-fold by 10 μM ABA (6 h), up to ~7-fold by drought (2–4 h) and ~2.25-fold by 200 mM NaCl (6 h). Database analysis (RiceXPro) indicates expression in multiple tissues with relatively higher levels in reproductive organs.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abscisic acid (ABA) treatment, 200 mM NaCl salt stress, drought (air-drying and water withholding), and gibberellin (GA3) treatment; proteasome inhibitor MG132 used in some interaction/localization assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of ABA signaling and ABA–GA crosstalk by a RING-H2 E3 ligase in rice, and its role in drought and salinity stress tolerance and growth regulation.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salinity); hormone response traits (ABA hypersensitivity, reduced GA sensitivity); growth and seed traits (shoot elongation, plant height, seed size and dormancy).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: OsRF1 transcript levels increased ~3.25-fold by 10 μM ABA (6 h), up to ~7-fold by 2–4 h drought, and ~2.25-fold by 200 mM NaCl (6 h) in 10-day-old shoots. Endogenous ABA in OsRF1-OE shoots was on average 3.1-fold higher than WT under control, 2.82-fold higher under drought, and 2.92-fold higher under salt stress. Under hormone-free 1/2 MS, total shoot length of OsRF1-OE seedlings was ~58.2% greater than WT, while ABA (3 μM) almost completely inhibited OsRF1-OE shoot growth. Leaf and whole-plant water loss rates of OsRF1-OE were significantly lower than WT after 3–5 h of dehydration and during 1–3 days of imposed drought.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsRF1 overexpression causes growth retardation and a semi-dwarf phenotype in the paddy field, reduced seed size, and enhanced seed dormancy; OsRF1-OE seedlings display diminished GA-stimulated second sheath elongation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. Dongjin wild-type plants; OsRF1 overexpression lines (OsRF1-OE1, OsRF1-OE2, OsRF1-OE3; T2/T3); rice protoplasts for localization and interaction assays; recombinant GST-tagged OsRF1 expressed in E. coli.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Oryza sativa cv. Dongjin (also referred to as O. sativa L. cv. Dong-jin).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of 35S:OsRF1 overexpression lines in Oryza sativa cv. Dongjin via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; growth assays under ABA and GA3 treatments; drought (water withholding) and 200 mM NaCl salt tolerance assays; leaf and whole-plant water loss measurements using detached leaves and DroughtSpotter platform; LC-MS/MS quantification of ABA; qRT-PCR of OsRF1 and ABA biosynthetic genes; subcellular localization using GFP fusions and ER mCherry marker in rice protoplasts; yeast two-hybrid, BiFC and co-immunoprecipitation to detect OsRF1–OsPP2C interactions; in vitro self-ubiquitination and substrate ubiquitination assays; cell-free degradation assays for OsPP2C09; immunoblots of SLR1.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation primarily by OsRF1 overexpression in rice combined with ABA/GA growth assays and drought/salt tolerance tests, supported by ABA quantification, qRT-PCR of ABA biosynthetic genes, subcellular localization, Y2H/BiFC/co-IP with OsPP2C09, in vitro OsPP2C09 ubiquitination, and cell-free degradation assays.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified bioinformatically as a rice RING-H2 protein with similarity to AtXerico and OsRHP1, then cloned as a synthetic, codon-optimized ORF and functionally characterized by transgenic overexpression and molecular assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsRF1 is a promising transgenic or gene-editing target to improve rice tolerance to drought and salinity by boosting ABA-mediated stress responses and water conservation. However, constitutive overexpression causes semi-dwarfism and enhanced seed dormancy, indicating that breeding applications will likely require controlled expression (e.g., stress- or tissue-specific promoters) or fine-tuning OsRF1 activity to balance improved stress tolerance with acceptable growth and yield.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsPP2C09 corresponds to LOC_Os01g62760 and is described as a core negative regulatory component of ABA signaling in rice. OsPP2C09 shows nucleus-specific localization in prior work, and BiFC with OsRF1 confirms nuclear interaction signals. Previously characterized OsPP2C09-OE transgenic lines (from Min et al., 2021) were used here to analyze SLR1 protein stability under hormone treatments.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsPP2C09 is a clade A PP2C from rice that directly interacts with the RING-H2 E3 ligase OsRF1 in yeast and rice protoplasts.
2. OsPP2C09 is polyubiquitinated in vitro by OsRF1 and undergoes 26S proteasome-dependent degradation, which is enhanced by ABA and is faster in OsRF1-OE extracts than in WT.
3. Drought stress and ABA treatment further decrease OsPP2C09 protein stability, linking its degradation to stress-induced ABA signaling.
4. In OsPP2C09 overexpression lines, the DELLA protein SLR1 accumulates to lower levels under GA3 or ABA treatment than in WT, indicating that OsPP2C09 promotes GA-induced SLR1 degradation and participates in ABA–GA cross-regulation of growth.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsPP2C09 acts as a direct OsRF1 substrate whose ABA- and drought-enhanced, proteasome-dependent degradation relieves PP2C inhibition of ABA signaling, while increased OsPP2C09 expression promotes GA-induced SLR1 degradation, thereby modulating the balance between ABA-mediated stress responses and GA-driven growth.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Functions as a clade A PP2C phosphatase that negatively regulates ABA signaling; its protein stability is controlled by OsRF1-dependent polyubiquitination and 26S proteasome degradation, which is stimulated by ABA and drought. Elevated OsPP2C09 promotes GA-induced SLR1 degradation, thereby antagonizing the OsRF1/ABA-mediated stabilization of SLR1 and growth restraint.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling pathway (as a clade A PP2C negative regulator) and GA signaling via modulation of DELLA protein SLR1 stability, forming part of an ABA–GA cross-regulatory network together with OsRF1.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsRF1 --(E3 ligase-mediated polyubiquitination and 26S proteasome-dependent degradation)--> OsPP2C09; OsPP2C09 --(negative regulation)--> ABA signaling; OsPP2C09 --(promotion of degradation)--> SLR1 (DELLA) -->(repression)--> GA-responsive stem elongation and growth restraint.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade A protein phosphatase type 2C (PP2C; PP2CA).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nuclear localization indicated by BiFC signals of OsPP2C09-VYNE with OsRF1-VYCE appearing in the nucleus of rice protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsRF1 (RING-H2 E3 ligase): direct interaction confirmed by yeast two-hybrid, BiFC in rice protoplast nuclei, co-immunoprecipitation, and functional substrate relationship in in vitro ubiquitination and degradation assays.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment, drought stress, and GA3 treatment; MG132 used to block proteasome-mediated degradation in some assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of clade A PP2C OsPP2C09 as a direct E3 ligase substrate linking ABA signaling to GA/SLR1-mediated growth responses in rice, and its degradation-based regulation under drought and salt-related hormonal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Hormone signaling and growth regulation (ABA signaling, GA/SLR1-mediated stem elongation).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsPP2C09-overexpressing (OsPP2C09-OE) rice lines; wild-type rice seedlings; rice protoplasts; recombinant His-tagged OsPP2C09 expressed in E. coli.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Construction of OsPP2C09-HA and OsPP2C09-VYNE fusions for co-expression with OsRF1 in rice protoplasts; yeast two-hybrid using pGBKT7-OsRF1 and pGADT7-OsPP2C09; BiFC in rice protoplasts with MG132 treatment; co-immunoprecipitation of OsRF1-GFP and OsPP2C09-HA; expression and purification of recombinant His-tagged OsPP2C09 from E. coli; in vitro ubiquitination with GST-OsRF1, E1, E2 and ubiquitin; cell-free degradation assays of His-OsPP2C09 in protein extracts from ABA-treated OsRF1-OE and WT seedlings with or without MG132; immunoblot analysis of endogenous SLR1 in OsPP2C09-OE and WT seedlings under GA3 and ABA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC and yeast two-hybrid interaction assays with OsRF1, co-immunoprecipitation in rice protoplasts, in vitro ubiquitination and proteasome-dependent degradation assays, and analysis of SLR1 protein stability in OsPP2C09-OE rice seedlings under GA3 and ABA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified as a clade A PP2C; in this study the OsPP2C09 ORF (LOC_Os01g62760) was cloned for His-tagged recombinant protein production and for tagged constructs used in interaction and degradation assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because OsPP2C09 is a clade A PP2C substrate of OsRF1 that negatively regulates ABA signaling and promotes GA-induced SLR1 degradation, reducing OsPP2C09 activity or enhancing its OsRF1-mediated degradation could be used to strengthen ABA-dependent drought and salinity tolerance and increase SLR1-mediated growth restraint in stress-prone environments. Conversely, OsPP2C09 overexpression favors GA responses and growth at the likely cost of stress tolerance, suggesting it as a tunable node for balancing yield and resilience in rice breeding.
